question: [File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEntityBalanceQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes the selectTokenBalance subquery to use json_agg(jsonb_build_object(...)) over a large number of token rows per account, causing the database to allocate large JSON objects in memory for every account in the result set, exhausting database memory and causing a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getOneAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter that causes parseTimestampFilters() to generate a non-empty timestampRange, forcing getAccountQuery() to be called with isHistorical=true, which triggers both the entity UNION ALL and the entity_stake UNION ALL subqueries, doubling the number of table scans per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/accounts.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccounts()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /accounts?account.id=<id1>&account.id=<id2>&account.publickey=<key>&account.balance=gte:0&limit=<max>&order=desc to combine all accepted parameters simultaneously, creating the most expensive possible query that exhausts database CPU, I/O, and Node.js memory?
